LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSED THAT SHE AND MR. KISLIK ATTENDED THE SAME LAW SCHOOL. SHE DOES NOT BELIEVE THAT THIS WILL AFFECT HER ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL. THE COURT GAVE COUNSEL THE OPPORTUNITY TO ASK QUESTION. NO QUESTION OR OBJECTION WAS RAISED. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION (GENERAL NEGLIGENCE) IS GRANTED. IT IS DUPLICATIVE OF THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION AND THERE IS NO SEPARATE STATUTORY BASIS FOR LIABILITY FOR GENERAL NEGLIGENCE. THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION IS DENIED. THE CITY FAILS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN ON THE ISSUE OF CONSTRUCTIVE NOTICE, PARAGRPAH 14 OF THE UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACT 14 WITH THE SUPPORTING EVIDENCE OF THE ANDERSON DECLARATION AT PARAGRAPHS 11 AND 12 DOES NOT SHIFT THE BURDEN. EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS BY THE PLAINTIFF ARE OVERRULED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER). JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 (302/MJM)

Notice Of Motion, Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Sep-05-2014 Continued To Sep-24-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is continued to September 24, 2014, to give the opposing party a chance to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. The opposing party shall immediately provide a courtesy copy of the opposition papers with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/eg

Ntc Of Mtn, Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Jul-23-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Elizabeth Pederson, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3862, for defendant and moving party;
Steven L. Weiner, Esq. of the Law Office of Steven L. Weinter and Associates, Tel. 925.838.7510, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. For judicial economy, defendant's counsel asked the Court to take judicial notice of certain facts. The request was denied. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is denied. The grounds for a motion for judgment on the pleadings "shall appear on the face of the challenged pleading or from any matter of which the court is required to take judicial notice." (CCP sec. 438.) Where the Court is asked to take judicial notice, the particular matters to be noticed must be specified either in the notice of motion or supporting points and authorities. (Id., see also Weil & Brown, Cal. Prac. Guide: Civil Proc. Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2013) par. 7:315.) Defendant does not request that the Court take judicial notice of any facts. The alleged statute of limitations defect does not appear on the face of the complaint. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

RULING - JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSED THAT SHE AND MR. KISLIK ATTENDED THE SAME LAW SCHOOL. SHE DOES NOT BELIEVE THAT THIS WILL AFFECT HER ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL. THE COURT GAVE COUNSEL THE OPPORTUNITY TO ASK QUESTION. NO QUESTION OR OBJECTION WAS RAISED. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION (GENERAL NEGLIGENCE) IS GRANTED. IT IS DUPLICATIVE OF THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION AND THERE IS NO SEPARATE STATUTORY BASIS FOR LIABILITY FOR GENERAL NEGLIGENCE. THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION IS DENIED. THE CITY FAILS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN ON THE ISSUE OF CONSTRUCTIVE NOTICE, PARAGRPAH 14 OF THE UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACT 14 WITH THE SUPPORTING EVIDENCE OF THE ANDERSON DECLARATION AT PARAGRAPHS 11 AND 12 DOES NOT SHIFT THE BURDEN. EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS BY THE PLAINTIFF ARE OVERRULED. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order). Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
